summary: Maria Santos and Solutions Architect Kevin O'Brien met with Robert Langston and Tanya Desai (Security Engineering) to address a six-hour Detect platform outage and discuss contract renewal ahead of end-of-Q2. Kevin explained the root cause as a cascading single point of failure in the event ingestion pipeline and outlined remediation steps including redundant nodes and a circuit breaker pattern. Robert and Tanya outlined clear conditions for renewal justification: a formal written post-mortem, updated SLA language with uptime teeth for Detect, and a configuration review. The call also surfaced an upcoming Comply v2 launch with on-demand compliance reporting relevant to the customer's June PCI audit.
actionItems: Kevin O'Brien: Send technical brief with updated architecture diagrams to Tanya's team within one week
Maria Santos: Schedule a Detect configuration review with Tanya's security engineering team
Maria Santos: Deliver a formatted external post-mortem document to Robert by end of next week
Maria Santos: Obtain initial SLA proposal with updated Detect uptime terms from commercial team within two weeks

keyMoments.text: Robert expresses serious concern about a six-hour Detect outage and its risk exposure implications for a financial services firm
keyMoments.speaker: Robert Langston

keyMoments.text: Tanya calls out the single point of failure as a basic architectural problem, directly challenging the platform's engineering credibility
keyMoments.speaker: Tanya Desai

keyMoments.text: Robert discloses the board is asking questions about alternatives and that SentinelShield is being evaluated
keyMoments.speaker: Robert Langston

keyMoments.text: Tanya expresses genuine interest in the Comply v2 on-demand PCI DSS reporting ahead of their June audit, signaling a positive engagement opportunity
keyMoments.speaker: Tanya Desai
